skill.name: gpt-image-edit
skill.description: Edit images with OpenAI GPT Image 2 (the `/edit` endpoint of ChatGPT Images 2.0) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model. Documents GPT Image Edit's strengths (preservation language, multilingual in-image text editing, multi-reference up to 10 images, layout / typography precision), the schema, and when to route to Nano Banana Edit / Flux Kontext / GPT Image 2 t2i instead. Calls `runcomfy run openai/gpt-image-2/edit` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "gpt image edit", "gpt-image-edit", "chatgpt image edit", "edit with gpt image 2", or any explicit ask to edit with this model.
skill.summary: Image editing with GPT Image 2 on RunComfy — preserves identity, rewrites multilingual text, supports multi-reference composition
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate documentation wrapper for OpenAI GPT Image 2 via RunComfy CLI. All static analyzer findings are false positives: external_commands are markdown code examples, hardcoded URLs are documented service endpoints, path traversal flags are placeholder syntax, and weak crypto flags were incorrectly triggered. The skill documents proper security practices including token storage with 0600 permissions and input boundary handling. No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Edit Images with GPT Image 2 on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Creating multilingual image variants and precise edits requires multiple tools and iteration. This skill bundles GPT Image 2 prompting patterns so you get sharper output with preservation language, multilingual text editing, and multi-reference composition.
actual_capabilities: Edit images with preservation language to keep faces, poses, and brand elements unchanged
Rewrite multilingual text (Latin, kana, CJK, Cyrillic, Arabic) directly in images
Compose images from multiple references (up to 10) with directional spatial edits
Resize output with preserved aspect ratio or fixed dimensions (1:1, 2:3, 3:2)
Replace backgrounds, move headlines, swap CTAs with layout-precise positioning
limitations: Only 3 fixed size options plus auto — any other value returns a 422 error
Long compound prompts cause drift — split into multiple passes for complex edits
Batch consistency across many images performs better with Nano Banana Edit
Photorealism on portraits is stronger with Nano Banana Pro

use_cases.title: Multilingual Ad Localization
use_cases.description: Generate localized ad variants by replacing headline text while keeping photography, layout, and brand mark unchanged. Quote the exact text you want in each language.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and advertisers creating global campaigns

use_cases.title: Brand-Safe Product Photography
use_cases.description: Swap backgrounds or reposition product elements while preserving identity. Use preservation language to keep faces, poses, and branding consistent.

use_cases.title: Multi-Reference Composition
use_cases.description: Compose subjects from one image into scenes from another. Reference up to 10 images and use numbered cues to route editing to the correct source.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Background Swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the person/face, pose, and brand mark unchanged. Replace the background with [description of new background].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to change the setting while preserving identity

prompt_templates.title: Multilingual Text Replacement
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the photograph, layout, and brand mark exactly as in the input. Replace only the in-image headline. The new headline reads "[text]" in [language/script], same position and font weight as before.
prompt_templates.scenario: When creating localized versions of branded assets

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Composition
prompt_templates.prompt: Compose subject from image 1 into the scene from image 2. Match the lighting and color palette of image 2. Keep image 1 subject identity (face, pose, clothing) unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: When combining elements from multiple source images

prompt_templates.title: Layout-Precise Repositioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep [element 1], [element 2], and [element 3] unchanged. Move the headline from [original position] to [new position]. Adjust the layout to accommodate the new positioning.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need precise control over element placement

output_examples.input: Keep the main person, pose, and face identity unchanged. Replace the background with a soft warm-grey studio sweep and a gentle floor shadow.
output_examples.output: A professionally edited product photo with the subject isolated against a clean studio background, preserving all original facial features, pose, and any visible branding.

output_examples.input: Keep the photograph, layout, and brand mark exactly as in the input. Replace only the in-image headline. The new headline reads "咖啡优惠" in bold Chinese characters, same position and font weight as before.
output_examples.output: A localized advertising poster with identical layout and imagery, but the original English headline now displayed in Chinese with matching style and positioning.

output_examples.input: Keep the main product, packaging design, and label identity unchanged. Move the logo from the top-left corner to the bottom-right corner. Adjust the layout to accommodate the new logo position.
output_examples.output: The same product image with the logo repositioned to the bottom-right, maintaining all other design elements in their original positions.

best_practices: Lead with preservation goals — always state what to keep unchanged before describing edits. The model honors what is stated first.
Quote exact text instead of paraphrasing — use the exact characters you want when replacing multilingual text in images.
Use numbered references for multi-image edits — refer to images by number like "subject from image 1, lighting from image 2" for accurate routing.
anti_patterns: Do not include long compound instructions that change multiple unrelated elements — this increases drift. Split complex edits into separate passes.
Do not skip preservation language — the model may subtly rewrite faces, brands, or framing without explicit keep statements.
Do not paraphrase in-image text — saying "Japanese text about coffee" produces different results than quoting "コーヒー".

faq.question: How does this compare to Flux Kontext or Nano Banana Edit?
faq.answer: GPT Image Edit excels at multilingual text editing and preserving identity through edits. Flux Kontext is better for single-shot local edits with source fidelity. Nano Banana Edit handles batch consistency across many images (up to 20).

faq.question: Can I edit text in any language?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports Latin, kana (Japanese), CJK (Chinese/Korean), Cyrillic, and Arabic scripts. Always quote the exact characters you want in the output.

faq.question: How many reference images can I use?
faq.answer: Up to 10 publicly-fetchable HTTPS URLs. The first image is the primary input; subsequent images provide auxiliary cues for composition, lighting, or style.

faq.question: What happens if my prompt is too complex?
faq.answer: Long compound prompts cause drift — the model may not preserve all elements accurately. For complex edits, split into multiple passes focusing on one change at a time.

faq.question: How is my API token secured?
faq.answer: RunComfy CLI stores tokens in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with 0600 permissions (owner-only). For CI environments, use the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: What output sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Three fixed sizes: 1024x1024 (1:1), 1024x1536 (2:3 portrait), 1536x1024 (3:2 landscape), plus 'auto' which preserves the input aspect ratio.
